annotator S.report_text:

APT29 has been observed crafting targeted spearphishing campaigns leveraging web links to a malicious dropper An adversary may rely upon a user clicking a malicious link in order to gain execution.

once executed, the code delivers Remote Access Tools (RATs) and evades detection using a range of techniques. An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.

both groups exfiltrate and analyze information to gain intelligence value Adversaries may exfiltrate data.

These actors set up operational infrastructure to obfuscate their source infrastructure, host domains and malware for targeting organizations, establish command and control nodes, and harvest credentials and other valuable information from their targets. Obfuscation of files or information to evade detection.

These actors set up operational infrastructure to obfuscate their source infrastructure, host domains and malware for targeting organizations, establish command and control nodes, and harvest credentials and other valuable information from their targets. once executed, the code delivers Remote Access Tools (RATs) and evades detection using a range of techniques. An adversary may rely upon a user clicking a malicious link in order to gain execution.

exfiltrated email from several accounts Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over a different protocol than that of the existing command and control channel.

through encrypted connections back through operational infrastructure. Adversaries may employ an encryption algorithm to conceal command and control traffic rather than relying on any inherent protections provided by a communication protocol.
